name: Redondela – path through Monte San Vicente
name_original: Redondela – sendero por Monte San Vicente
region_intro: Hiking around Redondela offers diverse landscapes, blending coastal beauty with a rugged mountainous interior. The region features green hills, tranquil forests, and paths along the Ría de Vigo, providing hikers with views of the estuary and its iconic Rande Bridge. Trails often traverse varied terrain, including woodlands and ancient stone paths, with historical features visible from many routes. The area is a key point on the Portuguese Way of the Camino de Santiago, offering well-trodden paths through countryside and vineyards. Best hiking trails around Redondela The most popular hiking route is Panoramic view of Vigo – View of Ponte de Rande loop from Teis, a 18.1 miles (29.2 km) trail that takes 8 hours 27 minutes to complete, offering extensive views of the Ría de Vigo and its iconic bridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Redondela – path through Monte San Vicente, a difficult 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path that climbs through forested areas to elevated positions. Local hikers also love the Alto de Cabaleira from Cesantes — round trip, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a notable viewpoint, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Hiking around Redondela is defined by coastal paths, elevated viewpoints overlooking the Ría de Vigo, and peaceful forest trails. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Redondela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Redondela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Redondela?
A: Redondela offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Redondela?
A: Thanks to its mild, Atlantic-influenced climate, Redondela is pleasant for hiking year-round. However, spring, early summer, and autumn are particularly ideal, offering comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Redondela has several easy hiking options. There are 12 easy routes, often featuring gentle terrain and shorter distances, perfect for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375143767/alto-de-cabaleira-from-cesantes-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Cabaleira from Cesantes — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.4-mile trail leading to a notable viewpoint.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls in Redondela?
A: Yes, the region around Redondela features natural attractions including waterfalls. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Senda del Agua&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a refreshing natural highlight along some routes.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Redondela?
A: Hikers in Redondela are frequently rewarded with spectacular views. Many trails offer panoramic vistas of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4794959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vigo Bay and the Ría de Vigo&lt;/a&gt;, an estuary famously mentioned by Jules Verne. Elevated points like Alto da Cabaleira and Monte Penide provide stunning outlooks, especially at sunset, and you can often spot the iconic 19th-century railway viaducts.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites to see while hiking?
A: Absolutely. Redondela's trails offer a glimpse into the past. On Monte Penide, hikers can discover ancient rock art and a prehistoric necropolis. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondela Historic Center – Pilgrimage Site loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by significant landmarks, and the Roman milestone of Vilar de Infesta is another historical point of interest along the Camino route.

Q: Are there coastal walking routes in Redondela?
A: Yes, Redondela offers beautiful coastal paths. The walking path through A Portela, starting behind the Alameda garden, leads to the Ría de Vigo bay, showcasing the changing coastal landscape, small ships, and diverse birdlife. Many routes provide views of the Vigo Bay and the broader Ría de Vigo.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Redondela?
A: Many trails in Redondela are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and adhere to local regulations, especially in protected areas or near wildlife.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Redondela?
A: The hiking routes in Redondela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal views to tranquil forests, and the well-marked paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Redondela?
A: Yes, Redondela offers several circular routes for hikers. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40333959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view of Vigo – View of Ponte de Rande loop from Teis&lt;/a&gt;, a longer circular trail offering extensive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375143767/alto-de-cabaleira-from-cesantes-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Cabaleira from Cesantes — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trails using public transport in Redondela?
A: Redondela is a well-connected town, and many trailheads are accessible via public transport, particularly those closer to the town center or along major routes. For specific trail access, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or train connections to nearby villages.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Redondela?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In Redondela town, there are public parking areas. For trails further afield, smaller parking spots or roadside parking might be available near popular starting points. It's recommended to plan your route and check for designated parking areas in advance.
tips:
This garden is close to the Statue of Castelao and the Fountain of Santiago. The Alameda has been named after Castelao, a prominent nationalist intellectual and politician, since 19822.
In the centre of the garden, you will find a full-length sculpture of Castelao, funded by the neighbourhood and the Redondela Town Hall. It is an ideal place to stroll and enjoy nature and local history.🚶‍♀️😎🌄

Redondela is a wonderful place to visit! 
Also, Redondela is known for its local festivities such as the Festa da Coca and the Summer Carnival, which are events full of color and tradition. 🚶‍♀️😎🌄
Directly behind the old mill you will find a rustic spot with small waterfalls.

It is part of the Chan de Castiñeiras megalithic complex, made up of mámoas (ancient graves covered with earth forming a mound) and Neolithic dolmens.

This funerary monument, known as Mámoa do Rei, impresses with its size, twenty-five meters in diameter and two meters high, and is made up of two upright stone slabs buried and a third stone resting on the previous ones, placed horizontally. A perimeter ring of smaller stones delimits the mound, now cleared of earth so that its structure and interior can be clearly seen.
Great view, the hardships are rewarded.

The Parish Church of Santo André de Cedeira is a beautiful example of 18th-century Baroque architecture. Located in the parish of Cedeira, in Redondela, this church was designed by the renowned architect Fernando de Casas Novoa, who is also known for his work on the Obradoiro façade of the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela.
The cemetery attached to the church also has great historical value. At its entrance, you can read an inscription that reads: “Gloria eterna a cantos aquí xacen e que nos deron a vida, a terra e a lingua” (Eternal glory to all those who lie here and who gave life, land and language)⛪
